Source: Shadow Trace Ops Endpoint KQL Pack
WhyItMatters: Endpoint telemetry helps determine whether identity activity correlates with device logons, process execution, remote access, scripting, or network activity.
ReviewSteps: Identify devices where the target user logged on.
Review failed and remote interactive endpoint logons.
Check whether local admin or elevated logon indicators are present.
Pivot to process execution on the user's devices.
Review outbound network activity from devices used by the target user.
Look for suspicious scripting or living-off-the-land execution.
ExpectedResults: Rows may show endpoint logons, failed logons, remote access, process execution, or network activity associated with the target user or devices used by the target user.
NoResultsMeaning: No matching endpoint telemetry was returned, or Defender for Endpoint telemetry/tables are unavailable for the selected window.
RecommendedActions: Validate whether endpoint activity aligns with expected user behavior.
Pivot to device timeline in Defender XDR when suspicious results appear.
Review device onboarding and Advanced Hunting table availability if no endpoint telemetry is returned.
If malicious activity is validated, follow approved containment workflow.
